authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Cottonwood Lakes 1, 2, 3 and 4 and tributaries
reaches.fromDesc: Cottonwood Lakes Basin (John Muir Wilderness, Inyo National Forest)
reaches.toDesc: Cottonwood Lakes Basin (John Muir Wilderness, Inyo National Forest)

reaches.name: All remaining Cottonwood Creek drainage lakes
reaches.fromDesc: Upper Cottonwood Creek drainage lakes above Cottonwood Lakes 1-4 (e.g. Long Lake, High Lake, near New Army Pass)
reaches.toDesc: Upper Cottonwood Creek drainage lakes above Cottonwood Lakes 1-4 (e.g. Long Lake, High Lake, near New Army Pass)

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(33) — Cottonwood Creek drainage lakes (Inyo Co.)
sources.quotedText: (33) Cottonwood Creek drainage lakes (Inyo Co.). (A) Cottonwood Lakes 1, 2, 3 and 4 and their tributaries (Inyo Co.). Sep. 1 through Nov. 30. Only artificial lures may be used. Minimum size limit: 14 inches total length. 2 trout (B) All remaining Cottonwood Creek drainage lakes. All year. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(a)(4) — General possession limit provision for listed trout waters
sources.quotedText: Unless otherwise provided, it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit.

sources.title: Cottonwood Lakes Trail | Inyo National Forest | Forest Service
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Lake layer record for gaz_id 2130555, "Cottonwood Lake Number Three", Lake, Inyo County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":2130555,"gaz_name":"Cottonwood Lake Number Three","gaz_featureclass":"Lake","fcode":39004,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Inyo"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13160219.6639, 4369076.9688) converts to lon -118.220265, lat 36.495930, matching the file's recorded coordinate (-118.2203, 36.4959) to within ~30m — used as the anchor point for the Cottonwood Lakes 1-4 cluster (Lakes 1 and 2 are individually GNIS-named within ~600m; no CA/Inyo GNIS entry exists for a "Cottonwood Lake Number Four").

groups.humanSummary: Cottonwood Creek drainage lakes (Inyo Co.): Cottonwood Lakes 1-4 and tributaries open Sep. 1 - Nov. 30 only, artificial lures only, 14-inch minimum size, 2 trout; all remaining Cottonwood Creek drainage lakes open all year, artificial lures only, 2 trout.
groups.verbatimText: (33) Cottonwood Creek drainage lakes (Inyo Co.). (A) Cottonwood Lakes 1, 2, 3 and 4 and their tributaries (Inyo Co.). Sep. 1 through Nov. 30. Only artificial lures may be used. Minimum size limit: 14 inches total length. 2 trout (B) All remaining Cottonwood Creek drainage lakes. All year. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout

regulations.humanSummary: Cottonwood Lakes 1-4 and tributaries open September 1 through November 30 only

parameters.note: Only artificial lures may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: 14-inch minimum total length at Cottonwood Lakes 1-4 and tributaries
regulations.verbatimText: Minimum size limit: 14 inches total length.

parameters.counting_rule: Possession limit equals the daily bag limit (1x) per the general provision at 14 CCR §7.50(a)(4): unless otherwise provided, no more than one daily bag limit may be possessed.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 trout daily bag and possession limit at Cottonwood Lakes 1-4 and tributaries

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
